([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): 水野 竜也, [Hepburn](/wiki/Hepburn_romanization "Hepburn romanization"): *Mizuno Tatsuya*; born June 2, 1981) is a Japanese [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") currently competing in the [Middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight_(MMA) "Middleweight (MMA)") division. A professional competitor since 2006, he has fought for [DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(mixed_martial_arts) "Dream (mixed martial arts)"), [Pancrase](/wiki/Pancrase "Pancrase"), [DEEP](/wiki/Deep_(mixed_martial_arts) "Deep (mixed martial arts)"), [M-1 Global](/wiki/M-1_Global "M-1 Global"), [ProElite](/wiki/ProElite "ProElite"), [ONE FC](/wiki/ONE_Fighting_Championship "ONE Fighting Championship"), [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1"), and also participated in the [Dynamite!! 2010](/wiki/Dynamite!!_2010 "Dynamite!! 2010") event.
+ [Deep Middleweight Championship](/wiki/List_of_Deep_champions#Middleweight_Championship "List of Deep champions") (one time; former)
+ [2010 DREAM Light Heavyweight Grand Prix Runner Up](/wiki/Dream_16 "Dream 16")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Japanese kickboxer & mixed martial arts fighter (born 1981)

The native form of this [personal name](/wiki/Personal_name "Personal name") is *Mizuno Tatsuya*. This article uses [Western name order](/wiki/Personal_name#Western_name_order "Personal name") when mentioning individuals.

**Tatsuya Mizuno** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): 水野 竜也, [Hepburn](/wiki/Hepburn_romanization "Hepburn romanization"): *Mizuno Tatsuya*; born June 2, 1981) is a Japanese [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") currently competing in the [Middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight_(MMA) "Middleweight (MMA)") division. A professional competitor since 2006, he has fought for [DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(mixed_martial_arts) "Dream (mixed martial arts)"), [Pancrase](/wiki/Pancrase "Pancrase"), [DEEP](/wiki/Deep_(mixed_martial_arts) "Deep (mixed martial arts)"), [M-1 Global](/wiki/M-1_Global "M-1 Global"), [ProElite](/wiki/ProElite "ProElite"), [ONE FC](/wiki/ONE_Fighting_Championship "ONE Fighting Championship"), [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1"), and also participated in the [Dynamite!! 2010](/wiki/Dynamite!!_2010 "Dynamite!! 2010") event.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tatsuya_Mizuno&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Mizuno started competing in [judo](/wiki/Judo "Judo") in high school, and currently holds a 3rd *dan* [black belt](/wiki/Black_belt_(martial_arts) "Black belt (martial arts)") in the discipline. On April 29, 2005, he became the champion of fifth [Shooto](/wiki/Shooto "Shooto") East amateur mixed martial arts tournament, and on December 23, 2005, he took part in the U-FILE CAMP amateur tournament as member of Pankurasu geto.

On September 16, 2006, he defeated Yuji Sakuragi in his MMA debut at Pancrase: Blow 7.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He fought against the likes [Thiago Silva](/wiki/Thiago_Silva_(fighter) "Thiago Silva (fighter)"), [Assuério Silva](/wiki/Assu%C3%A9rio_Silva "Assuério Silva") and [Mirko Cro Cop](/wiki/Mirko_Cro_Cop "Mirko Cro Cop") early on in his career.

### DREAM

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tatsuya_Mizuno&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: DREAM")]

In 2010 Mizuno took part in the DREAM Light Heavyweight Grand Prix. He defeated [Melvin Manhoef](/wiki/Melvin_Manhoef "Melvin Manhoef") at [DREAM 15](/wiki/Dream_15 "Dream 15"), in the Light Heavyweight Grand Prix semifinal. In the final he fought with [Gegard Mousasi](/wiki/Gegard_Mousasi "Gegard Mousasi"), and lost to him by rear-naked choke submission.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Mizuno fought [Sergei Kharitonov](/wiki/Sergei_Kharitonov "Sergei Kharitonov") at DREAM's [Dynamite!! 2010](/wiki/Dynamite!!_2010 "Dynamite!! 2010") New Year's Eve event[[4]](#cite_note-4) and lost the fight via first-round KO.

On July 16, 2011, Mizuno defeated [Trevor Prangley](/wiki/Trevor_Prangley "Trevor Prangley") by KO (knee to the body) in the first round at [Dream: Japan GP Final](/wiki/Dream:_Japan_GP_Final "Dream: Japan GP Final"). Former UFC Middleweight [Yushin Okami](/wiki/Yushin_Okami "Yushin Okami") was present as his cornerman.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Post-DREAM

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tatsuya_Mizuno&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Post-DREAM")]

On January 21, 2012, Mizuno defeated Ilima Maiava via submission (arm-triangle choke) in the second round.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In June 2012, Mizuno faced MMA veteran [Renato Sobral](/wiki/Renato_Sobral "Renato Sobral") at [ONE Fighting Championship: Destiny of Warriors](/wiki/ONE_FC_Events#ONE_Fighting_Championship:_Destiny_of_Warriors "ONE FC Events"). Mizuno lost via [armbar](/wiki/Armbar "Armbar") submission in 31 seconds of the first round.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

He then faced fellow judoka Jason Jones at *[Glory 2: Brussels](/wiki/Glory_2:_Brussels "Glory 2: Brussels")* on October 6, 2012 in Brussels, Belgium.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.

Mizuno returned to the [One FC](/wiki/One_Fighting_Championship "One Fighting Championship") promotion on October 18, 2013 against Rafael Silva. Mizuno won a three-round unanimous decision.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In Mizuno's next bout, he faced undefeated [Brazilian](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") Leandro Ataides at [ONE Fighting Championship: Rise of Heroes](/wiki/ONE_FC_Events#ONE_Fighting_Championship:_Rise_of_Heroes "ONE FC Events") on May 2, 2014. Mizuno was stopped by strikes at 0:47 of round one.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Mizuno faced Brayan Rafiq at [ONE Fighting Championship: Battle of the Lions](/wiki/ONE_FC_Events#ONE_Fighting_Championship:_Battle_of_Lions "ONE FC Events") on November 7, 2014. He won the fight via unanimous decision.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Mizuno then faced João Batista Yoshimura at DEEP 98 on November 1, 2020.[[12]](#cite_note-12) He lost the fight via second-round knockout.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Mizuno made his first title defense attempt in a rematch against Yoshimura at DEEP 102 on July 4, 2021.[[14]](#cite_note-14) He lost the bout via second-round knockout.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Mizuno faced Seigo Mizuguchi on December 12, 2021 at DEEP 105 Impact, winning the bout in the first round after soccer kicking Mizuguchi.

Mizuno returned a year later to face Masashi Inada at DEEP 111 Impact on December 11, 2022, winning 19 seconds into the bout after dropping Inada with a knee and finishing him with ground and pound.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Mizuno faced Ryo Sakai for the Deep Super Heavyweight Championship on July 2, 2023 at DEEP 114 Impact, losing the bout in 42 seconds via TKO stoppage.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Championships and Accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tatsuya_Mizuno&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Championships and Accomplishments")]

* **[Deep](/wiki/Deep_(mixed_martial_arts) "Deep (mixed martial arts)")**
  + [Deep Middleweight Championship](/wiki/List_of_Deep_champions#Middleweight_Championship "List of Deep champions") (one time; former)
* **[DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(Mixed_Martial_Arts) "Dream (Mixed Martial Arts)")**
  + [2010 DREAM Light Heavyweight Grand Prix Runner Up](/wiki/Dream_16 "Dream 16")
